description: A candid color event photograph (no on-image text or graphic overlay) of a naturalization ceremony held at Dodger Stadium. A woman in a dark suit (USCIS Director Ur M. Jaddou) stands at a clear lectern with her right hand raised, administering the oath of allegiance. The lectern front bears the round seal of the U.S. Department of Homeland Security. American flags and a DHS/USCIS-colored flag flank the stage; tiered stadium seating filled with attendees forms the background, with 'DODGER STAD...' and 'SINCE...' signage visible. In the soft-focus foreground a person in military camouflage uniform raises a hand taking the oath, and a seated photographer/AV operator in sunglasses is mid-ground. This is a documentary photo of an official ceremony, not a designed meme or poster. It accompanies a celebratory tweet about 2,100 people from 120 countries becoming new U.S. citizens. Intent is positive PR around citizenship/naturalization; no slogan or designed typography on the image. From Aug 2022 (Biden-era).
notable_text: (none on image; incidental venue signage 'DODGER STADIUM' visible. Tweet celebrates 2,100 new citizens from 120 countries)
